product_goal: Provide a runnable B2B operations fixture that demonstrates UIdetox baseline-to-remediation work while preserving verified frontend, API, and database behavior.
audience: UIdetox maintainers, beta testers, and agent-harness evaluators
primary_job: Verify that UIdetox can map intent, correct structural and visual defects, preserve routes, and keep frontend operations aligned with API and database contracts.
preserve: React route paths and navigation destinations
FastAPI endpoint paths and typed response contracts
SQLite seed behavior and synthetic record lineage
Recorded pre-remediation issue and parity evidence
Synthetic provenance and the no-production-data guarantee
constraints: The fixture must install, typecheck, build, and run locally
Every visible action must work or state its local-only boundary
Frontend operations must remain aligned with backend methods and DTOs
Responsive layouts must avoid clipping, accidental overflow, and inaccessible controls
No production customer records, credentials, or third-party copyrighted UI assets
anti_goals: Do not treat NexusFlow as a production product or source of real business metrics
Do not reintroduce unsupported controls, fabricated claims, or optimistic mutation drift
Do not erase the evidence lineage that makes before-and-after qualification reproducible

lineage: hallmark intent, scope, and macrostructure preflight
provenance.visual_source_policy: No external product UI was copied; the initial AI-SaaS baseline and current editorial operations design are synthetic.
provenance.data_source_policy: All organizations, people, metrics, URLs, invoices, notifications, and events are fictional seed data.
